buildChecks.frontendTsc: PASS - npx tsc --noEmit produces no errors
buildChecks.backendTsc: PASS - npx tsc --noEmit produces no errors
buildChecks.viteBuild: PASS - vite build succeeds with 61 modules transformed, only non-blocking font asset warnings

accessibilityChecks.labelAssociation: All inputs have htmlFor/id pairs; checkboxes use fieldset+legend
accessibilityChecks.skipLink: govuk-skip-link present in GovLayout pointing to #main-content
accessibilityChecks.focusManagement: addField focuses new label input via setTimeout; deleteField returns focus to Add button
accessibilityChecks.errorSummary: GOV.UK error-summary with role=alert and aria-labelledby on all error states
accessibilityChecks.ariaLive: Loading states use aria-live=polite and aria-busy=true
accessibilityChecks.keyboardNavigation: All controls are native HTML (input, select, checkbox, button) - keyboard operable by default
accessibilityChecks.colorContrast: Relies on govuk-frontend which meets WCAG AA contrast ratios

govukCompliance.backLink: govuk-back-link rendered when backHref prop provided
govukCompliance.summaryCard: govuk-summary-card used for each field row

knownIssues: govuk-frontend JS modules (govuk-header, govuk-error-summary data-module) are not initialised via initAll() — visual behaviour is present but JS enhancements (skip-link polyfill, error-summary auto-focus) rely on CSS only. Not a WCAG blocker for hackathon scope.
Font assets from govuk-frontend show 'didn't resolve at build time' warnings in Vite — these are runtime paths embedded in the CSS and work correctly when served; not an error.
